name: Let-um Have It Eggplant
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel eggplants and cut into 1/2-inch thick circles.
2. Place in a large bowl with water, juice of one lemon, and 2 tbsp salt. Let sit until all pieces are covered.
3. Add ice cubes on top and set in refrigerator for about 1 hour.
4. In a separate bowl, mix 3 eggs, 1 cup milk, and granulated garlic.
5. Drain eggplants and pat dry with a towel.
6. Dip eggplant pieces into the egg mixture, then coat with bread crumbs.
7. Heat peanut oil in a frying pan until hot.
8. Fry eggplant pieces until golden brown, then transfer to paper towels and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese while still warm.
9. In a separate pan, melt 1/2 stick butter.
10. Add 1/4 tsp liquid crab boil and crab meat; cook for 2 minutes.
11. Melt remaining 1/2 stick of butter in the same pan.
12. Add 1/4 tsp liquid crab boil and shrimp; cook until pink.
13. Drain meats using a strainer.
14. For the sauce:
- In a Dutch oven, melt 1 stick of butter.
- Add white and green onions, bell pepper, and celery; cook for 3 minutes.
- Add garlic and mushrooms; cook for 2 minutes.
- Add flour and mix well.
- Break cream cheese into small pieces and add to the pan, blending until smooth.
- Add half-and-half, Tabasco, lemon juice, red, white, and black peppers, paprika, and Worcestershire sauce. Mix well and cook on medium until desired thickness.
- Add drained crab meat and shrimp; mix well.
15. Serve hot with fried eggplant pieces. Serves 6.
